question_text: Why do cyclists lean forward when cycling?

options.label: A
options.text: To increase their weight and go faster

options.label: B
options.text: To increase the surface area facing the air

options.label: C
options.text: To decrease the surface area facing the air and reduce air resistance

options.label: D
options.text: To create more friction with the ground

correct_answer: To decrease the surface area facing the air and reduce air resistance
explanation: When cyclists lean forward, they reduce the cross-sectional area of their body that faces the oncoming air. This smaller area means less air resistance acts against them, allowing them to cycle faster with less effort. Air resistance is a type of friction force that opposes motion through a fluid.
